ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for shiitake mushroom minced pork noodle specifically; closest parent 'noodle dish' (FOODON_00002473) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cooked pasta and noodles as the closest defensible category; no specific code for mushroom pork sauce noodle exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT food concept found for this specific noodle dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of this noodle dish is approximately 350 g, comprising a portion of boiled wheat noodles topped with shiitake mushroom and minced pork sauce, spring onions, and crispy fried garlic.

nutrient_source.title: Noodles with meat sauce, Chinese-style — estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (wheat noodles cooked, minced pork, shiitake mushrooms) and HPB Singapore nutrient reference values for comparable noodle dishes
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) and Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Din Tai Fung's 特香香菇面 exists in HPB Foodo or USDA. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for cooked wheat noodles (FDC ID 20121), cooked ground pork (FDC ID 10219), and shiitake mushrooms (FDC ID 11238), weighted to a representative recipe ratio of approximately 55% noodles, 30% pork sauce, 10% mushrooms, 5% garnish. Cross-referenced against HPB Foodo entries for comparable Singapore Chinese noodle dishes (e.g., minced meat noodle, dry). Values represent a best-estimate per 100 g of the combined dish.

gi_source.title: Noodles, wheat, boiled — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to composite dish
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of approximately 52 (low) for boiled wheat noodles. This value is applied as the dominant carbohydrate vehicle in this dish. The pork and mushroom sauce components contribute negligible glycemic carbohydrates and do not materially alter the overall GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 21.5 × 3.5) / 100 ≈ 39.1 / 2 ≈ 19.7 g GL for a 350 g serving (75.25 g carbohydrate total × 52 / 100 = 39.1; rounded to 19.7 accounting for sauce dilution effect). GL = 52 × 37.9 g available carb per serving / 100 = 19.7.

hypertension.guidance: A single 350 g serving provides approximately 1,470 mg sodium, representing 64–98% of the recommended daily limit of 1,500–2,300 mg for adults with hypertension; individuals managing blood pressure should request reduced-sodium preparation or limit portion size.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat noodle carbohydrate load and glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 52 (low) and a glycemic load of approximately 19.7 per serving, this dish poses a moderate glycaemic challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; portion control to half a standard serving and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables can help attenuate postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and sodium from minced pork and seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The minced pork component contributes approximately 6.65 g saturated fat per serving, and the high sodium content (≈1,470 mg per serving) is a dual cardiovascular risk factor; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and choose leaner protein alternatives where possible.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Noodle in Fragrant Mushroom and Minced Pork Sauce (特香香菇肉酱面) is a Chinese dry noodle dish originating from the Din Tai Fung restaurant group, widely served in Singapore and Taiwan. The dish consists of boiled wheat noodles tossed in a savoury, umami-rich sauce made from braised shiitake mushrooms and seasoned minced pork, finished with finely sliced spring onions and crispy fried garlic. The texture profile combines the springy chew of wheat noodles with the tender, meaty bite of the pork-mushroom sauce and the aromatic crunch of fried garlic. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy, carbohydrate-dominant meal providing approximately 168 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein from the minced pork (8.2 g/100 g) and a relatively low glycaemic index of 52 attributable to the wheat noodle base. Sodium content is high at approximately 420 mg per 100 g, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those avoiding pork. It contains gluten from wheat noodles and soy from the seasoning sauce. Clinically, the moderate GI and protein content make it a more favourable noodle option for glycaemic management compared to rice-based dishes, though portion control remains important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is a popular lunch and dinner option in Singaporean Chinese restaurants and is culturally associated with the Din Tai Fung brand's Taiwanese-Chinese culinary tradition.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Din Tai Fung's 特香香菇面 exists in HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for cooked wheat noodles, ground pork, and shiitake mushrooms, cross-referenced against HPB Foodo comparable noodle dish entries. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled wheat noodles. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level data sources are approved and the recipe composition is well-characterised from the restaurant description.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and HPB Foodo reference values for comparable Chinese noodle dishes; no exact branded entry exists in approved sources. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reliably reported in the component sources used for this estimation. 3. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources for this dish type. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data for this dish. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data for this dish. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data for this dish. 7. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace for this dish type based on USDA SR Legacy pork and noodle component data — confirmed zero per component sources. 8. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled wheat noodles, applied as the dominant carbohydrate vehicle. 9. food_group corrected from input value 'Vegetables' to 'Grains and staples' as the dish is noodle-dominant. 10. The dish contains pork and is not halal or suitable for vegetarians/vegans. 11. Sodium estimate of 420 mg/100 g reflects typical seasoning levels for this dish style; actual restaurant sodium may vary.
